2603.15526.title: Building Trust in PINNs: Error Estimation through Finite Difference Methods
2603.15526.paper_summary: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) constitute a flexible deep learning approach for solving partial differential equations (PDEs), which model phenomena ranging from heat conduction to quantum mechanical systems. Despite their flexibility, PINNs offer limited insight into how their predictions deviate from the true solution, hindering trust in their prediction quality. We propose a lightweight post-hoc method that addresses this gap by producing pointwise error estimates for PINN predictions, which offer a natural form of explanation for such models, identifying not just whether a prediction is wrong, but where and by how much. For linear partial differential equations, the error between a PINN approximation and the true solution satisfies the same differential operator as the original problem, but driven by the PINN's PDE residual as its source term. We solve this error equation numerically using finite difference methods requiring no knowledge of the true solution. Evaluated on several benchmark PDEs, our method yields accurate error maps at low computational cost, enabling targeted and interpretable validation of PINNs.
2603.15526.paper_summary_zh: 物理知識驅動的神經網絡（PINNs）是一種靈活的深度學習方法，用於解決偏微分方程（PDEs），這些方程模型涵蓋了從熱傳導到量子力學系統的現象。儘管具有靈活性，PINNs對於其預測如何偏離真實解的洞察有限，這妨礙了對其預測質量的信任。我們提出了一種輕量級的事後方法，通過為PINN預測生成點狀誤差估計來解決這一問題，這為這類模型提供了一種自然的解釋形式，識別不僅是預測是否錯誤，還有錯誤的位置和程度。對於線性偏微分方程，PINN近似與真實解之間的誤差滿足與原始問題相同的微分運算符，但由PINN的PDE殘差作為源項驅動。我們使用有限差分方法數值求解這個誤差方程，並不需要對真實解的了解。在幾個基準PDE上進行評估，我們的方法以低計算成本產生準確的誤差圖，實現了對PINNs的針對性和可解釋的驗證。
2603.15526.author: Aleksander Krasowski et.al.
2603.15526.authors: Aleksander Krasowski, René P. Klausen, Aycan Celik, Sebastian Lapuschkin, Wojciech Samek, Jonas Naujoks
